roles.role: Director of Data Science, Performance Marketing
roles.note: YOUR #1 ACTIVE ROLE and arguably the best of the whole search. Director title = the scope step-up you want, and the mandate is a bullseye on your wedge: lead the DS team running Root's performance-marketing engine — owning a NINE-FIGURE marketing budget, LTV-based bidding, incrementality/MMM measurement, experimentation. This is monetization + growth + experimentation + 0→1 leadership, exactly your story. Warm INBOUND from the hiring manager (Annie Yang sourced you on LinkedIn), not a cold app — a strong signal. Honest caveats: (1) company trajectory — Root is a small-cap public insurtech, but it just turned profitable (sub-100% combined ratio, record Q1'26), so the story is improving; (2) JD says PhD-preferred + full-funnel paid-acquisition experience — manage that gap by leaning on quant depth + actuarial-science degree + experimentation/monetization work. Frame everything as marketing spend → LTV-positive (profitable-risk) policies, not raw volume.
